sections.paragraph:
const. גְּאֹון, once pl. גְּאֹונִים (from the root גָּאָה), Eze 16:56, pr. elevation; hence-

(1) sublimity, majesty, of God, Exo 15:7 Isa 2:10, 19 , 21 24:14 , בִּגְאֹון יְיָ עָֽהֲלוּ “they sing with joy of the majesty of Jehovah.” Job 37:4, קֹול גְּאֹונֹו “his sublime voice,” thunder. Job 40:10, עֲדֵה־נָא גָּאֹון וָגֹבַהּ “deck thyself, now, with majesty and magnificence.” Mic 5:3.

(2) ornament, glory, splendour, Isa 4:2 60:15 , “I will make thee גְּאֹון עֹולָם a perpetual glory.” Isa 13:19, תִּפְאֶרֶת גְּאֹון כַּשְׂדִּים “the splendid glory of the Chaldeans,” said of the city of Babylon. Isa 14:11. Psa 47:5, גְּאֹון יַעֲקֹב “the glory of Jacob,” i.e. the Holy Land; also, God himself, Amo 8:7. גְּאֹון הַיַּרְדֵּן “the glory of Jordan,” poet. used of its green and shady banks, beautifully clothed with willows, tamarisks, and cane, where lions used to lie hid amongst the reeds, Jer 12:5 49:19 50:44 Zec 11:3 comp. Jerome on Zec. loc. cit.; Relandi Palæstina, page 274.

(3) i.q. גַּאֲוָה No. 3, pride, arrogance. Pro 16:18, “pride goeth before a fall.” גְּאֹון יַעֲקֹב “the pride of Jacob,” Amo 6:8 Nah 2:3 Job 35:12 Isa 13:11 16:6 . It is also ascribed to the waves, Job 38:11. Compare גֵּאוּת No. 4.
